rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Petition And Petition For Order To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Motion calendar for April 21, 2026, line 1. Amended Petition to Compel Arbitration is OFF CALENDAR. On February 27, 2026 the Court issued at tentative ruling requesting a supplemental brief addressing the propriety of service of the Petition pursuant to CCP 1011. No supplemental brief was filed providing authority or argument supporting the proposition that service of Petition to Compel Arbitration is appropriate pursuant to CCP 1011(a) (See original Proof of Service) or CCP 1011(b) (See Proof of Service attached to March 13, 2026 Declaration Regarding Service of Summons and Petition). Pursuant to Ex. A to the Declaration Regarding Service of Summons and Petition the service was purportedly "made in the manner specifically provided in particular cases." There is no argument or evidence referenced anywhere in the March 13, 2026 supplemental filing as to "the manner specifically provided" in this case and that the service as described in Exs. 2A-2C to the Declaration Regarding Service of Summons and Petition satisfies the specific requirement, whatever it may be. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
